pyrann

Contents:

  • pyRANN PACKAGE
  • pyRANN TUTORIAL
pyrann
  • pyRANN TUTORIAL
  • View page source

pyRANN TUTORIAL

This is a tutorial for plotting the descriptor space of a RANN database with UMAP as well as reducing the RANN database.

 1import pyrann as rann
 2import numpy as np
 3
 4# Create processing class instance with Mg RANN input file
 5processing = rann.processing('Mg.input')
 6
 7# Set processing instance features to RANN potential atomic descriptors
 8# Because the formalism is RANN, it also sets the Global Simulation Number,
 9#   Local Simulation Number, and Local ID for each atom
10processing.get_features()
11
12# Initialize UMAP instance for processing instance and return mapper
13mapper = processing.get_umap()
14
15# Plot UMAP embedding of atomic descriptor space with each atom colored by
16#   filename and rename the plot to "full_database.html"
17processing.plot(mapper, color_data=processing.filenames,
18                hover_info={'Global Simulation Number': processing.global_sim_num,
19                            'Local Simulation Number': processing.local_sim_num,
20                            'Local ID': processing.local_id},
21                key_str='full_database')
22
23# Reducing the database by 50%
24reduced = processing.reduce(percent=50.0)
25
26# Creating a boolean mask to only plot the reduced database
27subset_points = [True if i in reduced else False for i in processing.global_sim_num]
28
29# Plot UMAP embedding of atomic descriptor space of the reduced database with
30#   each atom colored byfilename and rename the plot to "reduced_database.html"
31processing.plot(mapper, color_data=processing.filenames,
32                hover_info={'Global Simulation Number': processing.global_sim_num,
33                            'Local Simulation Number': processing.local_sim_num,
34                            'Local ID': processing.local_id},
35                subset_points=subset_points,
36                key_str='reduced_database')
37
38
39# Get unique filenames in the order that they are read for feature calculation
40unique_filenames, unique_index = np.unique(processing.filenames,
41                                           return_index=True)
42unique_index = np.argsort(unique_index)
43unique_filenames = unique_filenames[unique_index]
44
45# Load dump files to a list of series instances in the order they are read for
46#   feature calculation
47series = [rann.load(i, series=True) for i in unique_filenames]
48
49# Get new series instances for reduced database
50global_count = 0
51reduced_series = []
52for i in range(len(unique_filenames)):
53    file_series = []
54    for j in range(len(series[i].systems)):
55        if global_count in reduced:
56            file_series.append(series[i].systems[j])
57        global_count += 1
58    reduced_series.append(rann.series(file_series))
59
60# Export reduced database to new dump files
61for i in range(len(reduced_series)):
62    reduced_series[i].export(f'{unique_filenames[i]}_reduced.dump',
63                             directory='reduced_DUMPS')
64
65# Getting the energy per atom for each atom for each simulation
66epa = [j.energy / j.natoms for i in series for j in i.systems for _ in range(j.natoms)]
67
68# Plotting the full database with atoms colored by energy per atom (eV/atom)
69processing.plot(mapper, color_data=epa,
70                color_type='linear',
71                hover_info={'Global Simulation Number': processing.global_sim_num,
72                            'Local Simulation Number': processing.local_sim_num,
73                            'Local ID': processing.local_id,
74                            'eV/atom': epa},
75                key_str='full_database_epa')
76
77# Plotting the reduced database with atoms colored by energy per atom (eV/atom)
78processing.plot(mapper, color_data=epa,
79                color_type='linear',
80                hover_info={'Global Simulation Number': processing.global_sim_num,
81                            'Local Simulation Number': processing.local_sim_num,
82                            'Local ID': processing.local_id,
83                            'eV/atom': epa},
84                subset_points=subset_points,
85                key_str='reduced_database_epa')

full_database.html

This plot is interactive. Hover over a datapoint to show the global simulation number, local simulation number, and local id for that particular atom. Click a legend entry to toggle between hide/show for that particular dump file.

reduced_database.html

This plot is interactive. Hover over a datapoint to show the global simulation number, local simulation number, and local id for that particular atom. Click a legend entry to toggle between hide/show for that particular dump file.

full_database_epa.html

This plot is interactive. Hover over a datapoint to show the global simulation number, local simulation number, local id, and energy/atom for that particular atom. There is a dual slider at the bottom left of the page. Adjusting this slider will remove atoms with energies outside of the slider range.

reduced_database_epa.html

This plot is interactive. Hover over a datapoint to show the global simulation number, local simulation number, local id, and energy/atom for that particular atom. There is a dual slider at the bottom left of the page. Adjusting this slider will remove atoms with energies outside of the slider range.

To reprduce the same plots on the same database using MTP descriptors, only lines 5 and 10 need to change.

 4# Create processing class instance with Mg MTP potential
 5processing = rann.processing('pot4_6.0.mtp')
 6
 7# Set processing instance features to MTP potential atomic descriptors
 8#   Setting file_fmt to 'dump' will convert all dump files in the directory
 9#   to cfg files in order to calculate MTP descriptors
10processing.get_features(file_fmt='dump')

full_database_mtp.html

This plot is interactive. Hover over a datapoint to show the global simulation number, local simulation number, and local id for that particular atom. Click a legend entry to toggle between hide/show for that particular dump file.

reduced_database_mtp.html

This plot is interactive. Hover over a datapoint to show the global simulation number, local simulation number, and local id for that particular atom. Click a legend entry to toggle between hide/show for that particular dump file.

full_database_mtp_epa.html

This plot is interactive. Hover over a datapoint to show the global simulation number, local simulation number, local id, and energy/atom for that particular atom. There is a dual slider at the bottom left of the page. Adjusting this slider will remove atoms with energies outside of the slider range.

reduced_database_mtp_epa.html

This plot is interactive. Hover over a datapoint to show the global simulation number, local simulation number, local id, and energy/atom for that particular atom. There is a dual slider at the bottom left of the page. Adjusting this slider will remove atoms with energies outside of the slider range.
